comments.author: Sparrow, Scott H. MAJ MIL USA ARSOUTH
comments.text: Highlighted items are particularly great places to insert your wisdom

anchors.excerpt: I pause from story-telling at this point to impart what little wisdom I have, as I will do throughout the book. The first “to do” is find out who you are, and where you came from. I know it’s not earth shattering advice and most readers will determine right now whether to read on

comments.text: Did I make this up or is it a real thing?

anchors.excerpt: My second grade teacher attempted quiet time; which worked only because if we were quiet enough, for long enough, she would fall asleep, and if we stayed quiet, she would stay asleep. Even 7 year old boys can be quite if it means getting away with mischief. There was always some

anchors.excerpt: I was not a lazy kid. I did great work when I enjoyed it and when I bought into the reason for it. I worked hard on the farm, often times riding my bike to the barn a mile and a half away to milk the cows without supervision before my age had double digits. I did make up games to

comments.author: Sparrow, Scott H. MAJ MIL USA ARSOUTH

anchors.excerpt: Grade school yielded to middle school and with middle school and with high school came the forging of the things that were important to me, the things that I valued.
Making decisions early and often that you will not take a little off the top in the organization will save you from embarrassment and maybe jail time….
